story.iWant: health check endpoints for the application
story.soThat: load balancers and monitoring systems can verify application health and database connectivity
story.tasks: - Install @nestjs/terminus package (^11.0.1) - Create HealthModule and HealthController in src/health/ - Implement GET /health endpoint (liveness check, always 200 OK, <10ms) - Apply @Public() decorator to bypass authentication - Implement GET /health/db endpoint (database connection test with Prisma.$queryRaw) - Return 200 OK when database connected, 503 when disconnected - Track and return response time in milliseconds - Implement GET /health/services stub endpoint for future expansion - Write comprehensive tests (unit + E2E) - Add Swagger documentation for all endpoints

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: AC-7.6.1: @nestjs/terminus package installed (version ^11.0.1) AC-7.6.2: HealthController created in src/health/ with HealthModule AC-7.6.3: GET /health endpoint returns { status: 'ok', timestamp: '...' } with 200 OK AC-7.6.4: GET /health endpoint is public (no authentication required via @Public()) AC-7.6.5: GET /health/db endpoint checks database connection (Prisma.$queryRaw SELECT 1) AC-7.6.6: GET /health/db returns { status: 'ok', database: 'connected', responseTime: 23, timestamp: '...' } when healthy AC-7.6.7: GET /health/db returns 503 Service Unavailable when database disconnected AC-7.6.8: GET /health/services endpoint created as stub for future expansion

doc.title: Epic 7 Technical Specification: Developer Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Complete AC specifications (AC-7.6.1 through AC-7.6.8), health check strategy (liveness vs readiness), performance requirements (<10ms for /health, <50ms for /health/db), Terminus integration details, and graceful failure handling patterns.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: Health check endpoints specification: GET /health (load balancer health check), GET /health/db (database connectivity check with response time), GET /health/services (comprehensive service health). Standard HTTP status codes for load balancer compatibility.

doc.title: Epics Breakdown
doc.snippet: User story definition, acceptance criteria for @nestjs/terminus installation, endpoint implementations (/health, /health/db, /health/services), public endpoint requirements, database connection testing, and response formats.

doc.title: Story 7.5: Sentry Error Tracking
doc.snippet: @Public() decorator available from Story 7.2 (bypasses JWT authentication), exception handling patterns established (SentryExceptionFilter, LoggingInterceptor), Winston logger available for health check failure logging, testing patterns (mock services, E2E tests).

artifact.reason: EXISTING health module - currently imports FilesModule for S3 health check. MUST BE MODIFIED to add Terminus integration and database module for Story 7.6.
EXISTING health controller with S3 health check only. MUST BE EXTENDED to add /health (liveness), /health/db (database), and /health/services endpoints for Story 7.6.
USE THIS DECORATOR on all health endpoints to bypass JWT authentication. Sets IS_PUBLIC_KEY metadata checked by JwtAuthGuard.
USE THIS SERVICE for database health check. Extends PrismaClient, provides $queryRaw method for SELECT 1 query to test database connection.
IMPORT THIS MODULE in HealthModule to access PrismaService for database health check.

REFERENCE for testing patterns: Mock service dependencies (S3Service.testConnection), test success/failure scenarios, verify response structure and status codes. Apply similar patterns for database health tests.

node.package: Health check framework for NestJS, provides HealthCheckService and health indicators
Core NestJS decorators and utilities (Controller, Get, Injectable)
Prisma ORM client for database queries ($queryRaw)
Structured logging (optional for health check failure logging)
Testing utilities for unit and E2E tests
HTTP testing library for E2E tests

story-context.constraints: - Health endpoints MUST be public (use @Public() decorator from Story 7.2) - GET /health MUST NOT call database or external services (liveness check only) - GET /health MUST return 200 OK always (unless application crashed) - GET /health response time MUST be <10ms (performance requirement) - GET /health/db MUST use Prisma.$queryRaw with SELECT 1 query - GET /health/db MUST measure and return response time in milliseconds - GET /health/db MUST return 200 OK when database connected, 503 when disconnected - GET /health/db response time MUST be <50ms for healthy database - Database health check SHOULD timeout after 5 seconds to prevent hanging - Health check exceptions MUST be caught internally (return 503, not 500) - NO sensitive information exposed in health check responses - Load balancer compatible: Use standard HTTP status codes (200, 503) - Testing: Unit tests MUST mock PrismaService.$queryRaw - Testing: E2E tests MUST test success (200) and failure (503) scenarios - Testing: E2E tests MUST verify public access (no JWT token required) - Swagger documentation MUST be added for all endpoints (@ApiOperation, @ApiResponse) - HealthModule MUST import TerminusModule and PrismaModule - HealthModule MUST be imported in AppModule

interface.name: GET /health
interface.notes: Basic liveness check, always returns 200 OK, no database or external service calls, public endpoint (no auth)

interface.name: GET /health/db
interface.signature: GET /health/db → { status: 'ok' | 'error', database: 'connected' | 'disconnected', responseTime: number, timestamp: string, error?: string }
interface.notes: Database readiness check, executes SELECT 1 query via Prisma.$queryRaw, returns 200 OK when healthy or 503 when unhealthy, public endpoint

interface.name: GET /health/services
interface.signature: GET /health/services → { status: 'ok' | 'degraded' | 'error', services: { database: { status: 'healthy' | 'unhealthy', responseTime: number }, ... }, timestamp: string }
interface.notes: Comprehensive service health check, currently returns database health only, extensible for future services (Redis, S3, Sentry), public endpoint

interface.name: PrismaService.$queryRaw
interface.notes: Execute raw SQL query, use for SELECT 1 to test database connection, measure execution time for health check response

interface.name: @Public() decorator
interface.notes: Marks routes as public (bypass JWT authentication), sets IS_PUBLIC_KEY metadata, checked by JwtAuthGuard, apply to all health endpoints

tests.standards:
Unit Testing: Use Jest framework, mock PrismaService with jest.spyOn() to control database query behavior (success/failure scenarios), test HealthController methods in isolation, verify response structure and status codes, target 90%+ coverage.
E2E Testing: Use supertest library, test actual HTTP endpoints (/health, /health/db, /health/services) with app.getHttpServer(), verify public access without JWT token, test database connection success (200 OK) and failure (503) scenarios, measure response times, verify error handling.
Test file locations: Unit tests in src/health/__test__/health.controller.spec.ts, E2E tests in test/health.e2e-spec.ts. Follow existing test patterns from test/health-s3.e2e-spec.ts (mock service dependencies, test success/failure, verify response structure).
tests.ideas: AC-7.6.1: Verify @nestjs/terminus package installed in package.json AC-7.6.2: Test HealthModule imports TerminusModule and PrismaModule AC-7.6.3: Test GET /health returns 200 OK with { status: 'ok', timestamp } AC-7.6.3: Test GET /health response time <10ms AC-7.6.4: Test GET /health accessible without JWT token (public endpoint) AC-7.6.5: Test GET /health/db executes Prisma.$queryRaw with SELECT 1 AC-7.6.5: Test response time measurement for database query AC-7.6.6: Test GET /health/db returns 200 OK when database connected AC-7.6.6: Test response includes { status: 'ok', database: 'connected', responseTime, timestamp } AC-7.6.7: Test GET /health/db returns 503 when database query fails AC-7.6.7: Test response includes { status: 'error', database: 'disconnected', responseTime, timestamp, error } AC-7.6.8: Test GET /health/services returns combined service status AC-7.6.8: Test services object includes database health E2E: Test concurrent health checks (no race conditions) E2E: Test health endpoints don't trigger authentication errors Performance: Test GET /health <10ms, GET /health/db <50ms
